author: s1na
content:
The interop event seems to have been fruitful on the history expiry front as well. Two meaningful updates:

* In-person discussions surfaced useful feedback on the `ere` format. One notable change is that ere filenames will encode a profile describing what is included (or excluded). Current profiles are `noproofs` and `noreceipts`. If you’re implementing it, definitely check out the spec updates and fixes from the past month. Excited as well that the spec PR has now been merged: https://github.com/eth-clients/e2store-format-specs/pull/16
* The parallel discussion around 786 has also led to this proposal: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11601. It defines how long of a reorg ELs should support. While its scope is state retention rather than block history, it feels highly relevant to the minimum block retention conversation given the overlap in target bounds and weak subjectivity considerations. I’d strongly encourage people here to take a look.

author: s1na
content: It feels like these conversations are increasingly converging, even if from different angles.
